According to studies, it is very evident that precursors in the cytotoxicity of carbon nanodots play a vital role in amyloid beta. One such study was proven by establishing a carbon nanodot in which the precursor taken was L-lysine, this resulted in more effective carbon nanodots than D-lysine having greater inhibition against the accumulation of amyloid beta. L-lysine-CDs have elevated membranes which leads to greater cytotoxicity [163,168–170].

In correspondence to amyloid beta, neurodegenerative disorders specifically Parkinson's disease show α-synuclein accumulation. Mature fibrils were mainly focused by graphene quantum dots which significantly precluded α-synuclein fibrillization, leading to a sudden decrease in α-synuclein that causes synaptic loss and neuronal death which eventually reduces mitochondrial dysfunction. Hence, resulting in an improvised neuron-to-neuron transmission of α-synuclein pathology, this confines the reduction of dopamine-mediated by α-synuclein [171,172]. Tan and coworkers fabricated a carboxylated single-walled carbon nanotube (SWCNT-COOH) to delay the release of levodopa (LD) for the treatment of Parkinsonism. Reaction of SWCNT-COOH nanohybrid and LD via π-π* stacking confirms the formation of SWCNT-COOH-LD which can be detected by Raman spectroscopy, transmission electron microscopy, field emission scanning electron microscopy, elemental analysis, FTIR spectroscopy for both SWCNT-LD and SWCNT-COOH nanohybrid. MTT assay was used to prepare the cell line of PC12 cells of Parkinson's disease to perform in-vitro studies to determine the effectiveness of nanohybrid. Therefore, SWCNT proved to be a potential agent as a carrier for drug delivery to the nervous system [173].

Various neurodegenerative disorders, specifically Alzheimer's disease is influenced by exposure to extreme levels of Cu2 + (cupric) ions which can result in oxidative injury in neurons through activation of accumulation of amyloid beta. However, it has been previously established that polymers alongside Nitrogen-loaded carbon nanodots are potentially able to combine with Cu-bound amyloid beta complexes. The reduction in amyloid beta accumulation and oxidative photogeneration of its peptides is observed due to this combination. Moreover, to amyloid beta accumulation, variations can also be done during the synthesis of amyloid beta in its molecular pathways. Thus, amyloid protein acts as the precursor and β-secretase 1 (BACE1) enzyme plays a crucial role in assisting the synthesis of amyloid beta. The generation of amyloid beta was decreased which eventually results in reduced fibrillation and toxicity of amyloid beta by using intended carbon nanodots that show extreme capability to restrain BACE 1. At the end it is clearly evident that carbon nanodots decrease the expression of amyloid beta thus, they prove to be a promising neurological agent in the treatment of Alzheimer’s disease. Analysis of different research validates for the therapy of many neurodegenerative disorders, functionalized carbon nanodots portray more advanced characteristics. Therefore, functionalized carbon nanodots assure to be a potential candidate for neuro-targeted drug carriers, that can invade brain tumors by freely crossing the blood–brain barrier. Fig. 6C showing mechanism of different carbon nanodots for treating Alzheimer’s disease and Parkinsons disease [174–177].

Carbon nanodots have tremendous physicochemical properties such as their remarkable biocompatibility, smaller size range making them easily absorbable into solid tumors, greater surface-volume ratio, and especially allowing internalization of different compounds owing to their abundant surface functional elements for targeting different biomarkers. Thus, making carbon nanodots a potential candidate for the purpose of drug and gene delivery. A combination of such properties with their potential for bioimaging can be efficiently used for theragnostic applications [178].

Platinum-based drugs are the most beneficial in the treatment of cancer which are employed more than 50 percent in chemotherapy. Oxaliplatin is a platinum-based anticancer drug that is used as an agent for theragnostic application, it is visualized through carbon nanodots photoluminescence, and through chemical coupling the prodrug that oxidizes into a variant of oxaliplatin is placed on the surface of carbon nanodots as a conjugate. The conjugated pro drug-CDs via endocytosis are taken up by tumor cells which provide an environment that is highly reducing leading to the reduction and release of the drug oxaliplatin. The time of injection and dose administration of the drug can be modified for effective results by closely monitoring the signals of the drug-conjugated carbon nanodots through their photoluminescence as high intensity gives an idea about the distribution of the drug and its response. A novel platinum (II) complex of oxaliplatin has also found its application recently as a potential pharmacotherapeutic agent for metastasized colorectal cancer. But Pt (II) complexes pose problems relating to drug resistance and side effects, thus substituting them with Pt (IV) complex as a prodrug (OXA (IV)–COOH). Zheng and coworkers synthesized carbon nanodots through thermal pyrolysis of polyene polyamine and citric acid to incorporate oxaliplatin. Oxaliplatin (IV) complex (OXA (IV)–COOH) was integrated on the surface of carbon nanodots via a condensation reaction, in which the Oxa was modified to have the COOH group to carry out the reaction between the amino group of CDs and the carboxyl group of (OXA (IV)–COOH) through chemical coupling. The Oxa-CDs were employed in liver cancer i.e., the hepatocarcinoma 22 (H22) cell line through injection in Chinese Kun Ming (KM) mice, and the drug distribution was monitored by in-vivo imaging. As a result, after two therapy sessions of Oxa-CDs (0.72 mg ml−1 of PT, 20 μl), 82.4 % of the H22 cell line tumor was decreased. Moreover, owing to the fluorescence of carbon nanodots, the course of therapy also gives an idea about the modifications required in the dose of the drug and time of injection. Fig. 6D shows the linkage between CDs and Oxa (IV) COOH [179].

Karthik and coworkers also fabricated drug CDs and monitored their distribution through the photoluminescence property of carbon nanodots, the CDs surface was integrated with photosensitive quinolones to determine the photoluminescence monitored release of the drug [61].

The carbon quantum dots are also employed for magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) drug delivery. One such example is, Fe3O4/CQDs coated CNTs with the help of 808 nm laser irradiation were utilized in photothermal and photodynamic therapy. This generated system was doped with Doxorubicin (DOX) to be applied as a drug delivery system. The sgc8c aptamer was also in conjugation with the system. This developed nano vehicle was employed for MRI and targeted fluorescence imaging. Also, the laser irradiation after incubation of the nano vehicle with the lung cancerous cells, it was reported that the nano vehicle was capable of killing most of the cancerous cells [181]. In another study, Wang and coworkers also reported that DOX-doped CQDs are highly capable to be employed in cancer therapy and cell imaging [182]. Moreover, the graphene-mesoporous silica platform was also developed through chemical adaptations which can be developed into an efficient carrier for drug delivery in cancer treatment. Mesoporous silica when coated with graphene nanosheets, and altered with a targeting peptide, was doped with DOX. Through chemophotothermal interdependent glioma therapy, it was assessed as a nanodevice for drug delivery. Aggregation of nano complexes inside the tumor cells was evidently elevated by adaptation of peptide while the drug release was achieved by pH and thermal changes [183].

Hyaluronic acid (HA) adapted and gated by graphene quantum dots hollow mesoporous carbon nanoparticles (HMCN) based multifunctional nano platform was designed by Fang and coworkers to envelope DOX which is an anticancer drug. Overexpression of the CD44 receptor in tumor cells was targeted through chemo photothermal therapy. Collaboration of GQDs and HMCN of an equal size i.e., 120 nm results in HA-HMCN(DOX)@GQDs nanocarriers which in NIR irradiation efficiently produce heat that increases the complementary photothermal output. Also, NIR irradiations result in improvised antitumor properties during in-vivo studies of HA-HMCN(DOX)@GQDs [184,185].

Carbon nanodots were fabricated through renewable sources by Ghosh et al., which were then conjugated with poly amido amine (PAMAM) dendrimers of different generations. CDP3 is one of the conjugates of PAMAM-CD which shows exceptional gene protection and ability of complexation against digestion through enzymes. Triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) gene treatment is possible through them because of the blood compatibility, reduced toxicity, and more effective gene transfection a promising gene delivery system is assured as shown in Fig. 7 A. Moreover, CDP3 detects Cu+2 ion with 93 % fluorescence dampening ability proving CDP3 to be highly discriminating in the identification of Cu ion even at 2 mm concentration which can be applied to detect metastatic stages of TNBC that indicate excessive Cu+2 ion levels [186].

Carbon nanodots owing to their tremendous physicochemical properties show the potential to remarkably control osteogenic differentiation, bone tissue regeneration and effective cell proliferation. The research mainly focuses on the structural characteristics of different carbon nanoparticles and their specific uses whether it is 0D (fullerenes), 1D (nanotubes of carbon), or 2D (graphene or its derivative). Stages on carbon nanomaterials (CNM) based or CNM-modified with excellent biocompatibility and bio-physicochemical properties have proven to enhance the osteogenic differentiation and guided regeneration of bone in bone tissue engineering. Also, owing to their spherical structure, the biomedicine field has also applied fullerenes as free radical scavengers and the exceptional fluorescence tracing ability of carbon dots which can be employed to study osteogenic intracellular differentiation process, Carbon nanotubes have a highly functionalizing nature that increases the capability for its bone repairing utilization, Graphene dots owing to large area of surface leads to π-π stacking that adsorbs the osteogenic cells, proteins having high effectiveness and a great loading capability, drug hydrogen bonding and dug electrostatic interactions. Together these abilities of CNMs can be utilized in tissue repairing and regeneration of bone as represented in Fig. 7B [187,188].

The carbon quantum dots having photoactive hydrophobicity/ poly-urethane nano complexes were studied for properties like anti-bacterial, photocatalytic activity, biocompatibility, and structural characteristics by Kovacova and coworkers. They fabricated these nanocomposites via the swell encapsulating-shrink method. Later, it was established through irradiation of blue light of low power that these CQDs/ nanocomposites are more efficient producers of singlet oxygen. Antibacterial experiments on E. Coli and Staphylococcus aureus with these nanocomposites showed bactericidal effects in 60 min of irradiation. Eventually, after 180 mins. Very strongly dyed degradation was established through blue light irradiation of hydrophobic CQDs/ poly-urethane nanocomposites [189].

Thakur and coworkers fabricated an antibiotic in conjugation with the carbon nanodots, Ciprofloxacin was conjugated with the CDs through the microwave-assisted process in which Gum Arabic (GA) was taken as the precursor to be employed as a theragnostic agent to control the release of drug, increased antimicrobial ability and bioimaging. Ciprofloxacin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic having excellent microbial activity over Saccharomyces cerevisiae was connected to the surface of CQDs. Thus, Cipro-CQDs exhibit greater biocompatibility on Vero cells than that of ciprofloxacin alone and also, the release of ciprofloxacin from CQDs majorly depends on physiological conditions. Cipro-CQDs significantly showed controlled release of drug and elevated antimicrobial activity towards gram-positive and gram-negative microbes. The live imaging of yeast cells displayed bright green colored fluorescence under the fluorescence microscope [190].

Chen et al., discovered a new antibacterial mechanism, where graphene quantum dots/ silver nanoparticles hybrids were employed as an antibacterial agent. However, Chen and coworkers were the first ones to establish that the GQD/AgNP hybrid disclosed the dual enzyme activity similar to peroxidase and oxidase. Along with peroxidase, GQD/AgNP hybrids were used to perform the same activities like catalysis of H2O2 decomposition to OH and with oxidase also it was used for similar activity of helping in the formation of reactive oxygen species (ROS). While acknowledging ROS has elevated antibacterial activity, GQD/AgNP hybrids against gram-positive (S. aureus), gram-negative (E. coli), and drug-resistant bacteria showed magnificent antibacterial activity. However, GQDs in several studies exhibit antioxidant activity, producing evident free radicals along with their relations with sp2 hybridized structures or their specific compositions [191]. Antibacterial activity by some carbon nanodots is exhibited by either the formation of ROS or the disintegrating of bacterial cell membranes. One such example is cationic carbon nanodots that are fabricated by pyrolysis of spermidine portraying effectual antibacterial ability against S. aureus via disintegration of the bacterial membrane, which were found to be beneficial in the treatment of Staphylococcus aureus induced keratitis of rabbits [192].

For international and national security concerns, surveillance and identification of explosives mesmerized the attention of the whole globe. Dinitrotoluene (DNT), picric acid and trinitrotoluene (TNT) are widely known explosives that pose a significant threat to human life, even in minute quantities. So, it’s a daunting venture for scientists to detect such chemical explosives. Generally, high-priced technologies are used in the detection of such explosive substances, but carbon dots are promising candidates which provide a cost-effective and efficient way to utilize them in selective surveillance and detection of explosive substance [193–195]. Zhang et al., stated that N-doped carbon dots hold the potential to detect TNT even in a minute quantity via by photoluminescence quenching method [196].

Cayuela et al., stated that Nitrogen-doped carbon dot-based sensors can be utilized for the detection of explosives which arise from a polymer of amphiphilic nature, Dodecyl-grafted poly (isobutylene-alt-maleic-anhydride) via the one-step method. Detection of 2,4-dinitrotoluene, 2,4,6-trinitrotoluene and other nitro aromatic explosives was successfully demonstrated from soil samples [197].

Light-emitting diodes are solid-state lighting appliances which transform electrical energy into light energy. Because of their various applications including full-colour displays, illuminating appliances and liquid crystal displays, they are seen as an interesting topic in the research field. Due to adjustable photoluminescence, stabilized light-emitting, environment and cost-effective nature, carbon dots are rising material for light-emitting diodes, and due to this the traditional toxic-metal related semiconductor quantum dots or costly rare earth-related phosphors are predicted to get replaced by carbon dots. Normally, carbon dots behave as phosphors in carbon dot-related LEDs. CDs dispersed homogeneously in matrix substances like silica, inorganic salts, transparent polymers (for example, polystyrene, PVA) or starch to avoid accumulation-caused quenching of carbon dots at powder or solid-state arising from π − π* direct interactions or immoderate energy of resonance. White or multi-colour carbon dot-based LEDs have been manifested by these approaches. However, these common approaches necessitate the utilization of procedures involving multi-steps and because of the thickness of solid matrix layer fluorescence can be depressed from the chip and expenses can be raised [127,198–201].

Carbon dots-based photoluminescence LEDs can be compared to quantum dots-based LEDs on the basis of both color rendering index and luminous efficiency. The applications of carbon dots are limited in high-performance optoelectronic gadgets because carbon-related LEDs show wide emission with full width of half maximum surpassing 80 nm [202].

Highly efficient carbon-based catalysts can be manifested by exclusive photoelectrochemical characteristics of carbon dots. The research related to the catalytic design of carbon dots has expanded in recent years. For various reactions, Carbon dots indicate highly catalytic action and alternatively, for high-performance electrocatalytic and photocatalytic design they behave as functional components [203].

On the basis of distinct properties, purposes or structures catalysts can be classified as electrocatalysts, photocatalysts and photo electrocatalysts. Photocatalysis has evolved as apparent application to create clean and renewable energy and better solar energy. Carbon dots are utilized as photocatalysts for reducing carbon dioxide, degrading of pollutants (metal ions, dyes, etc.), chemical reactions and splitting water in the methodology of conversion of solar energy, which derive advantages from cost-effective, from UV to NIR, separation of charge, superb photostability, broad light range of absorption and capability of transferring [204].

Moreover, carbon dots can show photoinduced electron transfer characteristics and up-converted photoluminescence which captivates more interest in developing photocatalysts via carbon dots. Yu et al., synthesized carbon dots derived TiO2 (CDs/P25) nano-composites for effective photocatalytic advancement of hydrogen within irradiations of both visible light and UV light. When UV light falls on CDs/P25, CDs behave as reservoirs of electrons and effective separation between light-induce pairs of electron-hole gets improved. However, CDs behave as sensitizers and stimulate the excitation of electrons in P25 when visible light irradiation falls on the CDs [205].

Inorganic or organic pollutants can be separated from water by membrane separation and adsorption, but complete degradation of pollutants is not possible by these methods additional disposal is required. So, by treating with photocatalysts, environmental pollutants may completely degrade or mineralized. Photocatalytic degradation technique is manifested as desirable technique for treating wastewater. CDs possess the promising capability for utilization in photolytically degrading environmental pollutants because of these characteristics including outstanding chemical stability, cost-effectiveness, eco-friendly and water solubility, which is elevated to conventional photocatalytic substances like TIO2, and ZnO. Also, CDs possess outstanding and adaptable optoelectronic and photo-fluorescent properties, as well as CDs are outstanding electron donors and acceptors, which can effectively divide holes and electrons. Hence, CDs can be utilized as a sole photocatalyst, photocenter, spectral transformer, catalytic centre, photosensitizer and electron mediator in the photocatalytic design [206].

A humongous amount of research is going on carbon dots in the nanomedicine and nanotherapeutics domain, and they have shown promising results, and are anticipated to flourish in the near future. Carbon dots are ideal candidates for attaining immense attention by researchers due to the properties they possess, including their superior biocompatibility profile, stability characteristics, the optical and electrical properties, having wide variety of potential applications in diagnosis and theragnostic. Furthermore, the fact that the cost-effective and rapid synthesis of these materials with low toxicity property make it more beneficial, the green synthesis aspect also gives rise to a new friend of the environment.

Due to the advent of technology, like that of 3D printing in the scaffold manufacture, controlled porosity scaffolds, increased bio-resorbability may become attainable, nevertheless prior to moving forward into large-scale serious clinical studies, NCDs’ concentration needs to be evaluated and optimization of passivating agents which are being used in animal model as well as cell cultures, so as to comprehend the long term effects and establish theses NCDs as a potential nanomedicine [207]. Moreover, the research on carbon dots may also be expanded to its integration of mobile sensing systems on smartphones or other portable and wearable devices to achieve fast medical applications [145].
This all-together could open new horizons of opportunities and replacement of traditional medicines with carbon dots as novel nanomedicines as shown in Fig. 7C.

Through this review, we have discussed the currently established research developments of carbon nanodots and their types, synthesis, physicochemical properties, applications and their effective role in the diagnosis and treatment of diseases. The synthesis process of carbon nanodots is quite effortless and cost-effective. Moreover, the magnificent optical and physical properties of carbon nanodots are excellent luminescence, exceptional biocompatibility, excessive quantum yields, low photobleaching, high photostability, low toxicity, higher potential for surface functionalization and doping, extensive chemical stability and many more. These properties are significantly exploited for various biomedical applications of bioimaging, biosensing, drug and gene delivery, antibacterial, antimicrobial, antioxidants, cancer diagnosis and their subsequent treatment and their role in the treatment of neurodegenerative disorders like Alzheimer and Parkinson's disease.
There is no conflict of interest and disclosures associated with the manuscript.
